epic.priority: bdo's arc, blessed and authorized in session 2026-06-19 ('Bless and auth given'), recorded here so sessions inherit it from the record, not from chat (his standing rule). A COMPOSING arc: it does not re-build the merge-node or loop/digest.py (epic.owner-harness), the off-log atom gate (.github/), the gateway economy (§13.10), or the namespace-join groundwork in loop/pull.py — it names the surface where deployment, acceptance, and version-promotion converge and adds only what none of them covers. Where a piece overlaps an existing epic's piece, its glue cites that piece and refuses to double-build (§10).
epic.arc: Work moves through named, gated environments as SNAPSHOTS. The arc began as a plain question — would three deploy environments and recorded GitHub Deployments help, even just toward the requirement that someone else accepts your work (D-2)? — and the evidence answered yes: the recurring failure mode is work with no clean checkpoint between 'built' and 'accepted' (13 stranded branches and a run of 'merged PR, but the cut is held — commits not on origin/main' in a single session's garden report), which is the same per-atom pipeline ↛ per-PR git namespace gap loop/pull.py was chipping at. bdo's reframe is the spine: a snapshot is the natural unit a second party accepts — immutable, named, recorded, the way an atom's identity already IS the sha256 of its bytes — and a deployment is the act of promoting a snapshot from one environment to the next, with acceptance attaching to the snapshot rather than the mutable branch. Two axes are named apart: the DEPLOY axis (where a served version is promoted to — preview, staging, production, each promotion a gated recorded deployment) and the RUNTIME axis (where the loop itself runs — local viewport, web/mobile remote, cloud agent — and the parity that makes the paved pens behave the same on all three).
epic.context: Today there is one environment (github-pages) and main auto-deploys to the live URL on every push (.github/workflows/pages.yml) — the one promotion that happens with no owner stamp, on the surface bdo actually judges. Two guardrails bound the arc. (1) Do not cargo-cult dev/staging/prod onto the LOOP substrate: it is local files, the log is truth, there is nothing to deploy — environments live only on the served and runtime surfaces (avoids the 1.0-on-AI-native category error). (2) A deploy gate executes bdo's stamp and never invents a verdict — the .github/ law: a server surface enforces TOWARD truth, never as a second authority. Production-promotion becomes the same shape as confirm-arc: bdo confirms, the node lands. Design captured in environments.proposal.md.
epic.value: Your stamp reaches the one promotion it never reached — the live surface you judge on stops changing without you. Work stops stranding because acceptance attaches to a frozen named snapshot instead of a mutable branch, which is also the unit that joins the per-atom and per-PR namespaces. And every ritual works the same whether the loop runs on your machine, your phone, or in the cloud.
epic.horizon: Work moves as snapshots: every promotion across an environment is a frozen, recorded checkpoint a second party accepts — served and operable wherever you are.

phases.framing: The local → git → github substrate ladder a snapshot climbs (bdo, 2026-06-20: 'name its phases and work process ... local, git, and github level environments for working and deployment'). This is the SAME arc as the deploy-axis/runtime-axis above, described richly (bdo: 'these are the same thing described richly') — each tier is named by the substrate its environment lives on, and each tier has two FACES: a WORKING environment (where work happens) and a DEPLOYMENT environment (where a snapshot is promoted to / served). The deploy-axis rungs (preview → staging → production) live INSIDE the github tier; the runtime-axis (local / web / cloud) is the local↔github working spread. Nothing above is replaced; the grid is the buildable cut and exposes the unbuilt local & git foundation (github's wave-1 production-gate already shipped, PR #286). A snapshot is BORN in the local working env, FROZEN as the acceptance unit, and PROMOTED rung by rung; acceptance attaches to it at every promotion.

local.tier: where the loop runs and work is made — the viewport and per-work worktrees; nothing has left the machine yet. Lowest blast radius; the genuinely-new foundation whose absence caused the stranding this arc exists to fix.

working.status: have (claude/* worktrees + viewport, the fleet) + BUILD the snapshot primitive — the spine is born here
working.glue: The working env already exists as the worktree fleet; what is unbuilt is naming the SNAPSHOT it produces — a frozen, content-addressed reference resolvable to its atom(s) and its landed commit. The spine's natural home is the local working face: a snapshot is minted here before any promotion.

deployment.status: NEW — local tier, the first build after the snapshot primitive
deployment.glue: Serve the named snapshot on localhost (loop.web serve / http.server) for bdo to eyeball BEFORE anything leaves the machine — the local face of his live-URL judging surface ([[bdo-eyeballs-on-live-url]]), recorded as a local deployment event. Acceptance: a named snapshot can be served locally and the act is on the record as a promotion to the local deployment env. DO NOT double-build (§10): composes loop/web.py's render/serve, adds only the snapshot binding + the deployment record.

git.tier: where work integrates — branches and main; the version graph. The checkpoint between 'made' and 'served'.

working.status: claude/* branches (the branch-ritual / git pen) — already a named working env; named here, not re-built
working.glue: Sessions develop on claude/* branches and never push to main directly; the git pen and branch-ritual already make this a governed working env. This cell is named for completeness; its only new work is whatever runtime-parity (#245) requires to behave the same remotely.

deployment.glue: main becomes the 'integrated, not yet accepted' rung a snapshot is promoted FROM into the github deployment tier. The recorded GitHub Deployment per environment is the version-promotion ledger, log-shaped (append-only 'this snapshot was promoted to this environment, by this gesture'). Acceptance: a snapshot's promotion path local-preview → staging(main) → production is a readable, recorded sequence of deployment events. Bridges git→github.

github.tier: where work is accepted by a second party and served to the world — PRs, GitHub Environments, Pages, the served control plane. Highest blast radius; the deploy-axis rungs live here.

working.glue: The PR is the review/acceptance surface (have, via the PR pen + merge-node). The horizon piece is the authenticated surface bdo steers from on any device — REMODELED from a 'served inbox' because an inbox is a queue; the real piece is operable steering over the whole snapshot/environment economy (promote, accept, read the deployment ledger), not a notification list. DO NOT double-build (§10): composes epic.owner-harness's owner surface and the gateway economy's served seam. The boundary piece; its shape firms as the earlier pieces land.

deployment.status: production-gate SHIPPED (PR #286, done-line 0137; activation pending bdo's first promote); preview-env wave 2 — BUILD
deployment.glue: production-gate: the live URL only changes on bdo's gesture (a protected 'production' GitHub Environment whose promotion is the owner stamp — executes, never judges — replacing today's unstamped main→pages auto-deploy). preview-env: every PR/branch gets its own deployed snapshot URL so bdo eyeballs visual work before it lands. The deploy-axis rungs: preview → (staging = git tier) → production.

runtime_parity.glue: The paved pens (PR pen, git pen, the summon/confirm rituals) behave the same whether the session runs local, web, or mobile remote Claude Code — issue #245. Spans the local↔github working faces. Acceptance: a named pen ritual that fails or is unreachable from a remote runtime today succeeds identically there, proven once.

epic.work_process: Autonomous self-pacing loop (bdo's choice, 2026-06-20 — 'name its phases and work process, [seizing] a loop over it'). MAPE-K shape over the gap fold: SENSE the top unbuilt piece in build order (loop.gaps grain), PLAN by writing its done-line before code (§9.4), EXECUTE the build through the paved pens, then requisition an INDEPENDENT review (D-2 — earn your own acceptance first, but never cast the deciding one; no one signs their own line) and ready the PR for the merge-node (which the loop requisitions but cannot be). KNOWLEDGE is the append-only log. Self-paced via scheduled wakeups, not a fixed interval. The loop CARRIES pieces; it STOPS and surfaces at a gate's refusal or a tier boundary, where bdo confirms before the next tier — bdo steers arcs, the loop carries pieces (done-line 0028). Build order: LOCAL tier (snapshot primitive → local preview) → GIT tier (staging rung) → GITHUB tier (preview-env → served control plane), foundation-first; github's production-gate already shipped.

pieces.glue: Name and record the snapshot as the unit acceptance attaches to: a frozen, content-addressed reference that joins an atom's pipeline identity to a git/PR landing and a deployable artifact. DO NOT double-build (§10): this extends loop/pull.py's namespace-join groundwork and the per-atom↔per-PR carbon-copy the PR pen already carries (D-13, atoms_on_main) — it is the joining unit over them, not a second ledger. Acceptance: a snapshot can be named, resolved to its atom(s) and its landed commit, and accepted once as one thing.

pieces.status: NEW — local tier; first build after the snapshot primitive
pieces.glue: Serve the named snapshot on localhost for bdo to eyeball before anything leaves the machine; recorded as a local deployment event. The local face of [[bdo-eyeballs-on-live-url]]. Composes loop/web.py; adds only the snapshot binding + the deployment record (§10).

main becomes the 'integrated, not yet accepted' rung a snapshot is promoted FROM into production. The recorded GitHub Deployment per environment is the version-promotion ledger, log-shaped (an append-only history of 'this snapshot was promoted to this environment, by this gesture'). Acceptance: a snapshot's promotion path preview→staging→production is a readable, recorded sequence of deployment events.

Every PR/branch gets its own deployed snapshot URL, so bdo can eyeball visual work before it lands instead of waiting for main (his judging surface is the live URL). Acceptance: opening a PR that touches a served surface yields a reachable preview URL bound to that snapshot; closing/landing it reclaims the preview.

pieces.status: SHIPPED — PR #286, done-line 0137; activation (bdo's first promote) pending
pieces.glue: The live URL only changes on bdo's gesture. A protected 'production' GitHub Environment whose promotion is authorized by the owner stamp (the same authority as confirm-arc — it executes, it does not judge), replacing today's unstamped main→pages auto-deploy. Closes the one promotion that escapes the stamp. Its done-line was written before the build (§9.4).

The paved pens (PR pen, git pen, the summon/confirm rituals) behave the same whether the session runs local, web, or mobile remote Claude Code — issue #245. Acceptance: a named pen ritual that fails or is unreachable from a remote runtime today succeeds identically there, proven once.

The authenticated surface bdo steers from on any device — REMODELED from a 'served inbox' because an inbox is a queue; the real piece is operable steering over the whole snapshot/environment economy (promote, accept, read the deployment ledger), not a notification list. DO NOT double-build (§10): composes epic.owner-harness's owner surface and the gateway economy's served seam rather than re-deriving them. The boundary piece; its shape firms as the earlier pieces land.
